lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'Intel Itanium 2 is discontinued. Intel ended shipments July 2021; HPE ended support December 31, 2025. Duplicate of itanium-2 entry.', 'source': 'https://www.tomshardware.com/news/intel-kills-itanium-processors-chips-hpe,38540.html'}
notes: 64-bit EPIC architecture processor; primary subject of Perspective; assessed as ready for mainframe-class workloads; superior to Xeon in performance/scalability per interviewees; ultimately discontinued.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'IBM zSeries mainframe (z/Architecture) is actively developed and sold. The z900/z990 generation is long retired, but the z/Architecture platform continues with z16/z17. IBM invested $150B in US including mainframe manufacturing.', 'source': 'https://www.networkworld.com/article/3972719/ibm-aims-for-autonomous-security-operations.html'}
notes: IBM z990 and z900 mainframe systems; primary platform being considered for migration/surround/offload in favor of Itanium-based alternatives.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': "Intel Xeon server platform is actively developed and sold. Current generation is Xeon 6 (Granite Rapids). A core product line for Intel's data center business.", 'source': 'https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/xeon.html'}
notes: 32-bit Xeon cited as already handling migrated mainframe-class workloads in production for 1+ years; performance comparable to mainframe; Itanium 2 superior to Xeon for larger workloads.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'legacy-supported', 'notes': "COBOL remains in heavy use in banking, insurance, and government, processing ~70-80% of world's business transactions. Not used for new projects but actively maintained on mainframes. Reached TIOBE Top 10 in 2024.", 'source': 'https://zmainframes.com/zlog/cobol-in-2024-is-it-still-relevant-or-a-dying-language/'}
notes: Majority of mainframe applications cited by interviewees as involving only COBOL (and DB2); migration to Itanium reported as straightforward — recompile with no code changes.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'FORTRAN remains actively used in HPC, scientific computing, and climate modeling. Reached TIOBE Top 10 in 2024. Modern Fortran (2008/2018 standards) supports OOP and parallel computing. Active standardization and community.', 'source': 'https://www.paulnorvig.com/guides/modern-fortran-for-high-performance-computing.html'}
notes: Cited alongside COBOL as mainframe language easily migrated to Itanium; can be recompiled with no code changes.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'IBM Db2 (DB2) is actively supported. Db2 for z/OS receives continuous updates; Db2 for distributed platforms supported through 2027+. Same as ibm-db2.', 'source': 'https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/db2-distributed-end-support-eos-dates'}
notes: Primary mainframe database; migration to Itanium is straightforward per interviewees; DB2 on Intel multiprocessor more cost-effective than mainframe VM-based allocation.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'CICS Transaction Server for z/OS actively updated (v6.2 in June 2024 with continuous delivery). Core transaction processing middleware for IBM Z mainframes. Same as ibm-cics.', 'source': 'https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/ibm-cics-transaction-server-zos-56-61-and-62-are-updated-october-2024'}
notes: IBM mainframe transaction processing middleware; migration to Itanium requires significant but doable effort; along with DL/1 and assembler, represents harder migration path.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'legacy-supported', 'notes': 'DL/1 (Data Language/1) is the IMS database access language, still supported as part of IBM IMS but not used for new development. Legacy mainframe technology maintained for existing systems.', 'source': 'https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ims'}
notes: IBM hierarchical database/transaction system; cited as requiring significant effort for Itanium migration along with CICS and assembler.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'legacy-supported', 'notes': 'IBM Mainframe Assembler (HLASM - High Level Assembler) remains supported and used for performance-critical mainframe code. IBM actively maintains HLASM. Not used for new applications generally but maintained for existing code.', 'source': 'https://www.ibm.com/products/hlasm'}
notes: Mainframe assembler code; hardest to migrate to Itanium platforms; cited as one of the key technical barriers to full mainframe replacement.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'AMD Opteron is a discontinued x86 server processor line. Final Opteron CPUs were Piledriver-based 4300/6300 series (2012). Succeeded by AMD EPYC. Wikipedia confirms Opteron is discontinued.', 'source': 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opteron'}
notes: Cited by interviewees as less real-world-tested than Itanium for mainframe-class workloads; users do not view Opteron as comparable alternative to Intel-based platforms.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Linux on IBM Z (zLinux) / IBM LinuxONE is actively developed and supported. IBM LinuxONE 4 Express announced February 2024. Three certified distributions: RHEL, SUSE, Ubuntu. Growing use case for cloud-native on mainframe.', 'source': 'https://newsroom.ibm.com/2024-02-06-New-IBM-LinuxONE-4-Express-to-Offer-Cost-Savings-and-Client-Value-through-a-Cyber-Resilient-Hybrid-Cloud-and-AI-Platform'}
notes: Viewed by interviewees as less manageable and less performant than Intel-based alternatives; not seen as credible mainframe migration path.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Virtual machine technology on IBM Z mainframes (z/VM) is actively supported and used. IBM z/VM continues to receive updates. Core technology for running multiple Linux instances on mainframe hardware.', 'source': 'https://www.ibm.com/products/zvm'}
notes: Mainframe VM for allocating fractions of processors to applications; DB2 cost-effectiveness on Intel multiprocessor vs. mainframe VM cited as factor favoring Intel migration.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Data warehousing and analytics is a large, growing market. Evolved from traditional EDW to cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift). Active and fundamental to enterprise BI.', 'source': 'General market observation'}
notes: CPU-intensive workload category that delivers largest immediate payback on Itanium vs. mainframe; includes SAS and large-database analytics.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'TPC (Transaction Processing Performance Council) benchmarks (TPC-C, TPC-H, TPC-DS, TPC-E) are actively maintained and used by database vendors for performance comparison.', 'source': 'https://www.tpc.org/information/benchmarks5.asp'}
notes: TPC and other industry benchmarks cited as demonstrating that Itanium 2 absolute performance and scalability has met or surpassed virtually all commercial application workload requirements.
